lines.gurmukhi: ਬਨ ਫੂਲੇ ਮੰਝ ਬਾਰਿ ਮੈ ਪਿਰੁ ਘਰਿ ਬਾਹੁੜੈ ॥
lines.translit: ban foole manjh baar mai pir ghar baahurrai |
lines.english: The forest is blossoming in front of my door; if only my Beloved would return to my home!

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰੁ ਘਰਿ ਨਹੀ ਆਵੈ ਧਨ ਕਿਉ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਬਿਰਹਿ ਬਿਰੋਧ ਤਨੁ ਛੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: pir ghar nahee aavai dhan kiau sukh paavai bireh birodh tan chheejai |
lines.english: If her Husband Lord does not return home, how can the soul-bride find peace? Her body is wasting away with the sorrow of separation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਵਰੁ ਭਵੰਤਾ ਫੂਲੀ ਡਾਲੀ ਕਿਉ ਜੀਵਾ ਮਰੁ ਮਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: bhavar bhavantaa foolee ddaalee kiau jeevaa mar maae |
lines.english: The bumble bee is buzzing around the flowering branches; but how can I survive? I am dying, O my mother!

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਕਿਲ ਅੰਬਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੀ ਬੋਲੈ ਕਿਉ ਦੁਖੁ ਅੰਕਿ ਸਹੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: kokil anb suhaavee bolai kiau dukh ank saheejai |
lines.english: The beautiful song-bird sings, perched on the mango tree; but how can I endure the pain in the depths of my being?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਚੇਤਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਜੇ ਹਰਿ ਵਰੁ ਘਰਿ ਧਨ ਪਾਏ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: naanak chet sehaj sukh paavai je har var ghar dhan paae |5|
lines.english: O Nanak, in Chayt, peace is easily obtained, if the soul-bride obtains the Lord as her Husband, within the home of her own heart. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੈਸਾਖੁ ਭਲਾ ਸਾਖਾ ਵੇਸ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: vaisaakh bhalaa saakhaa ves kare |
lines.english: Vaisakhi is so pleasant; the branches blossom with new leaves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰਿ ਆਉ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਦੁਤਰ ਤਾਰੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਢੁ ਨ ਮੋਲੋ ॥
lines.translit: ghar aau piaare dutar taare tudh bin adt na molo |
lines.english: Please come home, O my Beloved; carry me across the treacherous world-ocean. Without You, I am not worth even a shell.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨ ਦੇਖੈ ਹਰਿ ਦੁਆਰਿ ਆਵਹੁ ਦਇਆ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dekhai har duaar aavahu deaa kare |
lines.english: The soul-bride yearns to see the Lord at her door. Come, O Lord, and take pity on me!

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਰਿ ਨ ਜਾਨਾ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਮਾਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮਹਲੁ ਪਛਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: door na jaanaa antar maanaa har kaa mehal pachhaanaa |
lines.english: I know that You are not far away; I believe that You are deep within me, and I realize Your Presence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀਮਤਿ ਕਉਣ ਕਰੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵਾਂ ਦੇਖਿ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਢੋਲੋ ॥
lines.translit: keemat kaun kare tudh bhaavaan dekh dikhaavai dtolo |
lines.english: Who can estimate my worth, if I am pleasing to You? I see You, and inspire others to see You, O my Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਹੁ ਜੇਠੁ ਭਲਾ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮੁ ਕਿਉ ਬਿਸਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: maahu jetth bhalaa preetam kiau bisarai |
lines.english: The month of Jayt'h is so sublime. How could I forget my Beloved?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਵੈਸਾਖੀਂ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: naanak vaisaakheen prabh paavai surat sabad man maanaa |6|
lines.english: O Nanak, finding God in Vaisakhi, the consciousness is filled with the Word of the Shabad, and the mind comes to believe. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਥਲ ਤਾਪਹਿ ਸਰ ਭਾਰ ਸਾ ਧਨ ਬਿਨਉ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: thal taapeh sar bhaar saa dhan binau karai |
lines.english: The earth burns like a furnace, and the soul-bride offers her prayer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨ ਬਿਨਉ ਕਰੇਦੀ ਗੁਣ ਸਾਰੇਦੀ ਗੁਣ ਸਾਰੀ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhan binau karedee gun saaredee gun saaree prabh bhaavaa |
lines.english: The bride offers her prayer, and sings His Glorious Praises; singing His Praises, she becomes pleasing to God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਮਾਣੀ ਨਿਤਾਣੀ ਹਰਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਿਉ ਪਾਵੈ ਸੁਖ ਮਹਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: nimaanee nitaanee har bin kiau paavai sukh mahalee |
lines.english: The bride is dishonored and powerless; how will she find peace without her Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੈ ਮਹਲਿ ਰਹੈ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਆਵਣ ਦੇਹਿ ਤ ਆਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: saachai mehal rahai bairaagee aavan dehi ta aavaa |
lines.english: The Unattached Lord dwells in His true mansion. If He allows me, then I will come to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾੜੁ ਭਲਾ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਗਗਨਿ ਤਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: aasaarr bhalaa sooraj gagan tapai |
lines.english: The month of Aasaarh is good; the sun blazes in the sky.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੇਠਿ ਜਾਣੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਜੈਸੀ ਕਰਮਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਗੁਣ ਗਹਿਲੀ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: naanak jetth jaanai tis jaisee karam milai gun gahilee |7|
lines.english: O Nanak, in Jayt'h, she who knows her Lord becomes just like Him; grasping virtue, she meets with the Merciful Lord. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਨਿ ਰਸੁ ਸੋਖੈ ਮਰੀਐ ਧੋਖੈ ਭੀ ਸੋ ਕਿਰਤੁ ਨ ਹਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: agan ras sokhai mareeai dhokhai bhee so kirat na haare |
lines.english: The fire dries up the moisture, and she dies in agony. But even then, the sun does not grow tired.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਤੀ ਦੂਖ ਸਹੈ ਸੋਖੈ ਅਗਨਿ ਭਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: dharatee dookh sahai sokhai agan bhakhai |
lines.english: The earth suffers in pain, parched and roasted in the fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਥੁ ਫਿਰੈ ਛਾਇਆ ਧਨ ਤਾਕੈ ਟੀਡੁ ਲਵੈ ਮੰਝਿ ਬਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: rath firai chhaaeaa dhan taakai tteedd lavai manjh baare |
lines.english: His chariot moves on, and the soul-bride seeks shade; the crickets are chirping in the forest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਗਣ ਬਾਧਿ ਚਲੀ ਦੁਖੁ ਆਗੈ ਸੁਖੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਾਚੁ ਸਮਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: avagan baadh chalee dukh aagai sukh tis saach samaale |
lines.english: She ties up her bundle of faults and demerits, and suffers in the world hereafter. But dwelling on the True Lord, she finds peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਵਣਿ ਸਰਸ ਮਨਾ ਘਣ ਵਰਸਹਿ ਰੁਤਿ ਆਏ ॥
lines.translit: saavan saras manaa ghan varaseh rut aae |
lines.english: In Saawan, be happy, O my mind. The rainy season has come, and the clouds have burst into showers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਦੀਆ ਮਰਣੁ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਨਾਲੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: naanak jis no ihu man deea maran jeevan prabh naale |8|
lines.english: O Nanak, I have given this mind to Him; death and life rest with God. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਸਹੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਪਿਰ ਪਰਦੇਸਿ ਸਿਧਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: mai man tan sahu bhaavai pir parades sidhaae |
lines.english: My mind and body are pleased by my Lord, but my Beloved has gone away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰੁ ਘਰਿ ਨਹੀ ਆਵੈ ਮਰੀਐ ਹਾਵੈ ਦਾਮਨਿ ਚਮਕਿ ਡਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: pir ghar nahee aavai mareeai haavai daaman chamak ddaraae |
lines.english: My Beloved has not come home, and I am dying of the sorrow of separation. The lightning flashes, and I am scared.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਨੀਦ ਭੂਖ ਕਹੁ ਕੈਸੀ ਕਾਪੜੁ ਤਨਿ ਨ ਸੁਖਾਵਏ ॥
lines.translit: har bin need bhookh kahu kaisee kaaparr tan na sukhaave |
lines.english: Tell me - without the Lord, how can I sleep, or feel hungry? My clothes give no comfort to my body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਜ ਇਕੇਲੀ ਖਰੀ ਦੁਹੇਲੀ ਮਰਣੁ ਭਇਆ ਦੁਖੁ ਮਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: sej ikelee kharee duhelee maran bheaa dukh maae |
lines.english: My bed is lonely, and I am suffering in agony. I am dying in pain, O my mother!

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਦਉ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲੀ ਭਰਿ ਜੋਬਨਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: bhaadau bharam bhulee bhar joban pachhutaanee |
lines.english: In Bhaadon, the young woman is confused by doubt; later, she regrets and repents.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾ ਸੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਕੰਤੀ ਪਿਰ ਕੈ ਅੰਕਿ ਸਮਾਵਏ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: naanak saa sohaagan kantee pir kai ank samaave |9|
lines.english: O Nanak, she alone is a happy soul-bride, who merges in the Being of her Beloved Husband Lord. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲ ਥਲ ਨੀਰਿ ਭਰੇ ਬਰਸ ਰੁਤੇ ਰੰਗੁ ਮਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: jal thal neer bhare baras rute rang maanee |
lines.english: The lakes and fields are overflowing with water; the rainy season has come - the time to celebrate!

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਰਸੈ ਨਿਸਿ ਕਾਲੀ ਕਿਉ ਸੁਖੁ ਬਾਲੀ ਦਾਦਰ ਮੋਰ ਲਵੰਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: barasai nis kaalee kiau sukh baalee daadar mor lavante |
lines.english: In the dark of night it rains; how can the young bride find peace? The frogs and peacocks send out their noisy calls.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਿਉ ਪ੍ਰਿਉ ਚਵੈ ਬਬੀਹਾ ਬੋਲੇ ਭੁਇਅੰਗਮ ਫਿਰਹਿ ਡਸੰਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: priau priau chavai babeehaa bole bhueiangam fireh ddasante |
lines.english: "Pri-o! Pri-o! Beloved! Beloved!" cries the rainbird, while the snakes slither around, biting.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਛਰ ਡੰਗ ਸਾਇਰ ਭਰ ਸੁਭਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਿਉ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: machhar ddang saaeir bhar subhar bin har kiau sukh paaeeai |
lines.english: The mosquitoes bite and sting, and the ponds are filled to overflowing; without the Lord, how can she find peace?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪੂਛਿ ਚਲਉ ਗੁਰ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਜਹ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਤਹ ਹੀ ਜਾਈਐ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naanak poochh chlau gur apune jeh prabh teh hee jaaeeai |10|
lines.english: O Nanak, I will go and ask my Guru; wherever God is, there I will go. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸੁਨਿ ਆਉ ਪਿਰਾ ਸਾ ਧਨ ਝੂਰਿ ਮੁਈ ॥
lines.translit: asun aau piraa saa dhan jhoor muee |
lines.english: In Assu, come, my Beloved; the soul-bride is grieving to death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝੂਠਿ ਵਿਗੁਤੀ ਤਾ ਪਿਰ ਮੁਤੀ ਕੁਕਹ ਕਾਹ ਸਿ ਫੁਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: jhootth vigutee taa pir mutee kukah kaah si fule |
lines.english: If she is plundered by falsehood, then her Beloved forsakes her. Then, the white flowers of old age blossom in my hair.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਮਿਲੀਐ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮੇਲੇ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਖੁਈ ॥
lines.translit: taa mileeai prabh mele doojai bhaae khuee |
lines.english: She can only meet Him, when God leads her to meet Him; she is ruined by the love of duality.
